en: Got in at noon, left at midnight. That's a long day. Good for you.
ko: 정오에 들어와서 자정에 나갔어요. 긴 하루였네요. 잘했어요.

en: Nothing wrong with a hard day's work.
ko: 힘든 하루 일하는 건 나쁘지 않아요.

en: So you say, a man came in
and he reached across the counter and quote,
he grabbed up the money from my register and run off. Yeah.
ko: 내 금고에서 돈을 집어들고 도망쳤어요. 맞아요.

en: And there was no one else in the store. Not at that time, no.
ko: 그때 가게에 다른 사람은 없었어요. 그때는 없었죠.

en: And since the camera system wasn't working at the time,
you're the only one who saw the perpetrator. Yeah, I guess so.
ko: 당신만 범인을 봤네요. 네, 그런 것 같아요.

en: Yeah, so this person came in and they bought something. I think it was an Almond Joy. They bought an Almond Joy.
ko: 그래서 이 사람이 들어와서 뭔가를 샀어요. 아몬드 조이였던 것 같아요. 아몬드 조이를 샀어요.

en: And when you rang them up,
that's when they snatched the cash from the register.
ko: 그때 그 사람이 금고에서 현금을 낚아챘어요.

en: Sounds like it happened pretty fast,
but you say you got a good look at him, correct? Yes.
ko: 하지만 당신은 그를 잘 봤다고 하죠, 맞죠? 네.

en: You must drink stronger coffee than I do
ko: 당신은 제가 마시는 것보다 더 강한 커피를 마시는군요.

en: because after 11 hours on the job,
